name: gl-importer-mcp
description: Import CSV/XLSX accounting data into QuickBooks Online or Xero via the Synder Importer API.
long_description:
Official MCP server from Synder. Exposes the Synder Importer REST API as 19 tools covering accounts, companies, settings, entities, fields, field-mapping rules, post-import rules, imports, status polling, results, cancel, and revert. Same engine that thousands of bookkeepers use through the Synder web app, now callable from Claude, Cursor, and any other MCP-compatible host.

**Typical flow:** Paste a CSV path, tell the model what entity you're importing (Invoice, Bill, JournalEntry, etc.), and the model walks the full pipeline — column-to-field mapping, validation, dry-run, commit. Mapping rules are persisted per company so the next import is one step.

Repo: https://github.com/SynderAccounting/gl-importer-plugin
Docs: https://importer.synder.com

importer_api_token.title: Synder Importer API Token
importer_api_token.description: Bearer token from importer.synder.com → Account → API Keys.

importer_base_url.title: Importer API Base URL
importer_base_url.description: Override for staging/test. Leave the default for production.

tools.name: account_get
tools.description: Returns the current Synder Importer account: email, name, status, subscription, and connected-company count. Call this first to verify the IMPORTER_API_TOKEN is valid.

tools.name: companies_list
tools.description: Lists all accounting companies connected to the Importer account (QuickBooks Online via 'intuit', Xero via 'xero'). Use the returned 'id' as companyId for downstream tools.

tools.name: settings_get
tools.description: Returns per-company import settings (dateFormat, document-number behavior, product/account auto-creation, duplicate-skip). Check dateFormat before importing — CSV date columns must match.

tools.name: settings_update
tools.description: Updates per-company import settings. Pass only the fields you want to change inside 'settings' (e.g. { dateFormat: 'dd/MM/yyyy' }). Fetch current values with settings_get first so you don't clobber unrelated fields. Returns the updated settings.

tools.name: entities_list
tools.description: Lists importable entity types for a company (Invoice, Bill, JournalEntry, Customer, Vendor, etc.). Use the returned 'name' as entityName for fields_get and import tools.

tools.name: fields_get
tools.description: Returns the field schema for an entity in a company: every field with its type, required flag, alternativeTitles, and predefinedValues. Use to build or verify a mapping before running an import.

tools.name: mappings_list
tools.description: Lists field mappings saved for a company. Each mapping is reusable across imports of the same entity type.

tools.name: mapping_create
tools.description: Creates a new field mapping for an entity (e.g. 'Journal Entry', 'Bill', 'Invoice'). Look up valid target fields with fields_get first. Returns the created mapping including its id.

tools.name: mapping_update
tools.description: Replaces an existing mapping in full. Fetch the current mapping with mappings_list first and send the whole desired shape — this is a PUT, not a patch. Returns the updated mapping.

tools.name: mapping_delete
tools.description: Deletes a saved mapping. Irreversible. Imports that referenced this mapping keep their historical record but new imports can no longer pick it.

tools.name: imports_list
tools.description: Lists recent imports for a company with status and timestamps. Use to find an importId for status / results / revert / cancel.

tools.name: import_status
tools.description: Returns a single import's current status and summary (total / succeeded / failed / warnings). Status lifecycle: SCHEDULED → IN_PROGRESS → FINISHED | FINISHED_WITH_WARNINGS | FAILED | CANCELED. FINISHED can transition to REVERTING → REVERTED.

tools.name: import_results
tools.description: Returns per-row results for a finished import. Filter by 'type' (INFO / WARNING / ERROR) to surface only failures. Paginated — defaults to 20 rows per page, max 100.

tools.name: import_execute
tools.description: Uploads a CSV/XLSX file and starts an import using an existing mapping. The MCP server reads the file from disk — pass an absolute path. Returns the created import object including importId. Status starts as SCHEDULED — poll import_status or use import_wait. Limits: .csv/.xlsx/.xls only, 50MB max.

tools.name: import_auto
tools.description: Uploads a file and asks the server to auto-map columns to the target entity. Set dryRun=true to get the proposed mapping back without creating an import — useful for showing the user what will happen and asking 'does this look right?' before committing. Set dryRun=false (default) to auto-map and import in one call.

tools.name: import_cancel
tools.description: Cancels a SCHEDULED or IN_PROGRESS import. Already-imported rows are NOT rolled back — use import_revert for that. Returns the updated import status.

tools.name: import_revert
tools.description: Reverts a FINISHED or FINISHED_WITH_WARNINGS import — deletes the QuickBooks/Xero entries the import created, using their live SyncTokens. Status transitions FINISHED → REVERTING → REVERTED. Confirm with the user before calling — irreversible from their perspective.

tools.name: import_wait
tools.description: Polls import_status until the import reaches a terminal state (FINISHED, FINISHED_WITH_WARNINGS, FAILED, CANCELED, REVERTED). Exponential backoff (2s → 1.5× → cap 30s). Default timeout 600s — if exceeded, returns { status: 'POLLING', importId, lastSeen } so the LLM can re-call. Includes a per-type result count summary on terminal states (INFO / WARNING / ERROR; null if the server didn't return totals).

tools.name: import_csv
tools.description: Happy-path CSV/XLSX importer. Two-step: first call (without confirmed) auto-resolves the company, uploads the file, and returns the server's proposed mapping plus any missingRequired fields — show this to the user. Re-call with confirmed=true (and the same filePath) to run the real import and poll until it terminates. Returns { stage: 'DRY_RUN' | 'DONE', importId?, status, summary?, proposedMapping?, missingRequired? }.
